name: Fix the density matrix tomography record as a permanent lossless snapshot

phase_0.domain_name: Fix the density matrix tomography record as a permanent lossless snapshot

VINIT.justification: starting point where no measurement has occurred, density matrix unknown

TANCH.justification: closed system boundary containing the permanent snapshot

AFWD.justification: forward evolution from measurement data to reconstructed state

AREV.justification: checking backward from snapshot to ensure fidelity with original measurements

CLINK.justification: chaining multiple measurement snapshots into comprehensive history

IMSCRIB.justification: the snapshot recognizes itself as the true density matrix representation

FSPLIT.justification: splitting tomographic data across different measurement bases for completeness

FFUSE.justification: recombining measurements from all bases to recover original density matrix

EVALT.justification: affirmative state when snapshot matches target density matrix

EVALF.justification: negative state when snapshot fails quality checks

ENGAGR.justification: simultaneous presence of multiple possible density matrices during measurement

IFIX.justification: irreversible record of the final validated density matrix

phase_2.split_input: density matrix ρ requiring complete tomographic measurement
phase_2.fuse_result: original density matrix ρ reconstructed losslessly

phase_3.void_description: state before any tomographic measurement - density matrix ρ is undefined
phase_3.true_description: tomographic reconstruction succeeded - snapshot contains validated density matrix
phase_3.false_description: tomographic reconstruction failed - measurements could not recover proper density matrix
phase_3.both_description: superposition state during measurement - density matrix exists in parallel realities

steps.domain_action: Begin in uninitialized state before tomographic measurement
Apply tomographic measurement protocol to extract basis-dependent data
Distribute measurements across multiple measurement bases to ensure completeness
Confirm measurements in basis A yield consistent partial information
Process measurements from each basis to build projection data
Chain measurement sequences together in temporal order
Validate that measurement process preserves quantum coherence
Reconstruct density matrix components from measurement statistics
Combine information from all measurement bases to recover full density matrix
Verify reconstructed density matrix passes physical validity tests
Apply lossless compression to preserve all quantum information
Link compressed density matrix to measurement provenance records
Hold superposition of all possible valid density matrices during validation
Perform final consistency checks against quantum mechanical constraints
Confirm snapshot accurately represents original quantum state
Prepare density matrix for permanent storage serialization
Create irreversible permanent snapshot of validated density matrix
Embed self-verification checksums to ensure long-term integrity
Initialize read-only access protocols for the fixed snapshot
Seal the fixed boundary around the validated permanent record
Connect snapshot registry to external verification systems
Establish self-identification link from density matrix to its own record
Second permanence marker ensuring append-only archival status
Final boundary confirmation closing the tomographic process

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Quantum tomography protocol compiler mapping measurement instructions to hardware commands
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Quantum-classical interface mediating between quantum state measurements and classical record systems
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Quantum memory storing intermediate measurement results and classical storage for permanent snapshots
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Tomographic measurement scheduling system coordinating basis selection and timing
phase_5.alfs_store: Quantum state archive serving as canonical reference for validated density matrix records

phase_6.cycle_cost: Quantum measurement decoherence during tomographic process
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Unknown quantum state with undefined density matrix requiring measurement
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Validated permanent snapshot containing complete density matrix information
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — Lossless reconstruction preserves all quantum information during measurement-to-record conversion

--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow

noncomputable def fix_the_density_matrix_tomography_record_as_a_permanent_lossless_snapshot_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Begin in uninitialized state before tomographic measurement)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑚)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Apply tomographic measurement protocol to extract basis-dependent data)
  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := 𐑚) (Distribute measurements across multiple measurement bases to ensure completeness) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (5 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [3] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Confirm measurements in basis A yield consistent partial information)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Process measurements from each basis to build projection data)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Chain measurement sequences together in temporal order)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Validate that measurement process preserves quantum coherence)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Reconstruct density matrix components from measurement statistics)
      -- F-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙))  -- F-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.F)
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 ⊙)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Combine information from all measurement bases to recover full density matrix)
  (.arrow ⊙ 𐑙 𐑾)  -- [9] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Verify reconstructed density matrix passes physical validity tests)
  (.arrow 𐑾 ⊙ 𐑱)  -- [10] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Apply lossless compression to preserve all quantum information)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑳)  -- [11] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Link compressed density matrix to measurement provenance records)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑱 𐑾)  -- [12] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Hold superposition of all possible valid density matrices during validation)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑳 ⊙)  -- [13] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Perform final consistency checks against quantum mechanical constraints)
  (.arrow ⊙ 𐑾 𐑾)  -- [14] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Confirm snapshot accurately represents original quantum state)
  (.arrow 𐑾 ⊙ 𐑭)  -- [15] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Prepare density matrix for permanent storage serialization)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [16] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Create irreversible permanent snapshot of validated density matrix)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑭 𐑾)  -- [17] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Embed self-verification checksums to ensure long-term integrity)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [18] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Initialize read-only access protocols for the fixed snapshot)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑾 𐑱)  -- [19] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Seal the fixed boundary around the validated permanent record)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑡 𐑠)  -- [20] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Connect snapshot registry to external verification systems)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [21] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Establish self-identification link from density matrix to its own record)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [22] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Second permanence marker ensuring append-only archival status)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [23] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Final boundary confirmation closing the tomographic process)
